data.title: Stock futures sink, yields tumble as China hits back with tariffs on U.S.
data.description:
The gloomy mood on Wall Street is expected to persist, with stock index futures plunging on Friday as China slapped a 34% tariff on U.S., intensifying fears of a U.S. recession and global trade war. 

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -2.9%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -2.9%, and Dow futures (INDU) -2.7%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 15 basis points to 3.90%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 17 basis points to 3.55%.

Two days after U.S. President Donald Trump announced sweeping tariffs against several countries, China has retaliated with 34% tariffs on all U.S. imports from April 10, reported by the country’s official Xinhua News Agency.

Nonfarm payrolls surprised to the upside in March, while unemployment edged up.

U.S. stocks were mauled Thursday, with the S&P 500 closing in a correction as President Donald Trump's reciprocal tariffs stoked fears of a global trade war. The S&P 500 closed at -4.8%, finishing its worst session since June 2020.

The selloff occurred after Trump late Wednesday detailed new tariffs that the U.S. is enacting on imports. The minimum baseline tariff will be 10%.

"The last 24 hours have been truly historic for markets, as the impact of the U.S. reciprocal tariffs cascaded across different asset classes, with no sign of letting up overnight," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"In terms of what happens now, the big question is how the U.S.’s trading partners might retaliate, as that will play a huge role in determining what the overall economic and market impact will be… On the back of all this, investors grew increasingly fearful about a potential US recession, with U.S. equities seeing their sharpest decline in years," Allen added.

Financial markets expected a significant tax increase from Trump. Yesterday’s reaction shows the tax increase was worse than anticipated. U.S. dollar weakness is telling. We often hear that when the U.S. sneezes the global economy catches cold. This is not the US sneezing. This is the U.S. cutting off its own arm, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

"For now, US recession risks have risen. Over the longer term, effective tariff rates should fall somewhat. Rerouting supply chains, shifting demand patterns, and the political and economic pressure on Trump to retreat should reverse some of this damage," Donovan added. 

Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell is expected to speak at the SABEW conference in Arlington, Virginia.

data.title: Canada's unemployment rate rises to 6.7% in March
data.description:
The unemployment rate in Canada reported at 6.7% in March, matching estimates and higher than the previous month's 6.6%.

The labor force participation rate in Canada decreased to 65.20% in March from 65.30% in February.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWC), (NYSEARCA:FLCA).

Currency: (USD:CAD) (CAD:USD)

data.title: J&J highlights Tremfya’s efficacy against psoriatic arthritis in Phase 3 trial win
data.description:
Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) on Friday announced a Phase 3 trial win for its anti-inflammatory therapy Tremfya, highlighting the antibody-based drug’s efficacy as a first-line option for patients with active psoriatic arthritis ((PsA)).

JNJ said that Tremfya, which is already approved in the U.S. for PsA, reached the main goal and a critical secondary endpoint in its Phase 3b APEX study involving patients with active PsA. The three-year trial enrolled PsA patients who hadn’t received biologic drugs for the condition and whose diseases were not adequately controlled despite standard therapies.

Based on the study’s topline data, the company said that APEX reached the primary endpoint of reducing signs and symptoms of the disease and a major secondary endpoint related to the reduction of structural damage progression.

As for tolerability, the company added that the data were in line with the drug’s established safety profile and there were no new safety signals.

"These new topline data highlight the importance of addressing both inflammation and structural damage at the source as early as possible,” noted Terence Rooney, Rheumatology Disease Area Leader at JNJ’s Pharma Division.

data.title: DuPont drops after China starts probe over alleged antitrust violation
data.description:
• DuPont (NYSE:DD) fell 8% in pre-market trading at least partly after China said it started a probe into DuPont China. 
  • China's State Administration for Market Regulation said Dupont China is suspected of violating the anti-monopoly law and the regulator has started a probe, according to a posting on the authority's website.
  • DuPont (NYSE:DD) didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's email request for comment.

data.title: Cintas CFO Mike Hansen to retire, Scott Garula appointed successor
data.description:
• Cintas (NASDAQ:CTAS) announced on Friday the retirement of Executive Vice President and CFO Mike Hansen, and the appointment of Scott Garula as the successor.
  • Shares were trading -0.87% pre-market at $203.07.
  • Hansen is set to transition to a new role as assistant to the CEO of the corporate identity uniforms and related business services provider, effective May 31.
  • Garula currently serves as president of Cintas' rental division.

data.title: Fabrinet upgraded to Neutral at B. Riley
data.description:
• Fabrinet (NYSE:FN) was in focus on Friday as B. Riley upgraded the electronic manufacturing services provider to Neutral from Sell.
  • “We still remain concerned about FN's 800G sales to Nvidia (NVDA), which could come under pressure as hyperscalers increasingly buy transceivers directly rather than through NVDA,” analyst Dave Kang wrote in a note to clients. “Complicating the [near-term] outlook is the tariff uncertainty. While the situation remains fluid, the tariff rate for Thailand is currently at 36%.” 
  • Kang tweaked his price target to $176 from $178 in conjunction with the upgrade.
  • While Fabrinet is not alone in the worries about tariffs on Thailand, (as competitors such as Innolight, Eoptolink and Lumentum (LITE) also have facilities there), it's still up for debate on how customers and suppliers will deal with the situation, Kang explained.
  • Nonetheless, Kang said “a significant amount of these risk factors” are likely in the stock, while the company has “several catalysts” to counter these risks, including improving telecom demand.

data.title: Scott Davido replaces Steven Cohen as AYR Wellness' interim CEO
data.description:
• AYR Wellness (OTCQX:AYRWF) said on Friday that, effective today, Steven Cohen has stepped down as the interim chief executive officer of the company.
  • The AYR board of directors has appointed Scott Davido as interim CEO.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Tradeweb posts record volume for March, Q1 amid heightened market volatility
data.description:
Electronic marketplace Tradeweb (NASDAQ:TW) announced on Friday a record volume for March and the first quarter, driven by heightened market volatility.

TW shares were trading +1.33% pre-market at $151.00.

The company's March trading volume reached $59.6T, compared to $50.3T in February.

Average daily volume for March stood at $2.71T, compared to $2.49T a month ago.

For the first quarter, trading volume reached $164.5T, while the average daily volume was $2.55T.

"Electronic trading remained sticky and resilient across asset classes on Tradeweb, as market share gains and heightened market volatility helped drive record volume for the month of March and for the first quarter," said CEO Billy Hult.

data.title: Neurocrine Biosciences names Sanjay Keswani as chief medical officer
data.description:
• Neurocrine Biosciences (NASDAQ:NBIX) said it has appointed Sanjay Keswani as its chief medical officer, effective June 2.
  • Keswani has more than 20 years of experience in the pharmaceutical industry and will succeed Eiry Roberts in the role of the chief medical officer.
  • Roberts served as the chief medical officer for the past seven years and will remain with Neurocrine in a strategic advisory role.

data.title: OPKO Health authorizes $100 million increase to share buyback program
data.description:
• OPKO Health (NASDAQ:OPK) said its board has authorized an increase of $100 million to the company’s existing common stock repurchase program.
  • The capacity of the current program now stood at $200 million.
  • The firm added ~$41 million of its common stock has been repurchased under the existing program since its authorization in July 2024.
  • The company added it will fund the buyback from existing cash and cash equivalents, and future cash flows.
